module_map.responsibility: Lexical tokenization. Converts SQL string into Token stream using dialect-aware tokenizer rules.
Syntax parsing. Builds AST from token stream using recursive descent parser. ~425 parse methods for different SQL constructs.
AST node types. Core.py defines Expr base class with arg_types. Submodules (string.py, functions.py, etc) define 500+ Expression subclasses (Soundex, Concat, etc).
Code generation. Converts AST to target dialect SQL via 450+ methods (soundex_sql, concat_sql, etc). Dialect subclasses override for dialect-specific variants.
SQL dialect support. 25+ dialects (postgres.py, bigquery.py, etc). Each defines custom Parser and Generator subclasses to handle dialect-specific syntax and functions.
Query optimization. 15+ passes (qualify, pushdown_predicates, simplify, etc). Transforms AST to optimized form via rule application. Heavy use of expressions module.
SQL execution engine. PythonExecutor evaluates optimized AST against data. Integrates schema, table types, and planner for execution context.

pipeline_trace.key_function: Expr (base class with arg_types dict mapping child names to optional flags)
types_produced: Expression tree with parent/child links, arg_key tracking

pipeline_trace.key_function: optimize(expr: Expr, schema: Schema, rules: Sequence) -> Expr
types_produced: Optimized Expr tree (same types, transformed structure)

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central hub: all AST nodes inherit from Expr. Parser, generator, optimizer all depend on it. Defines foundational arg_types contract and tree traversal.
Dialect registry and abstraction. Parser and Generator import for dialect-specific subclassing. Optimizer uses dialect context for type inference and transformations.
Tokenizer integration point. Heavy use of TokenType enum. References all expression types. Dialect Parser subclasses override parse methods for syntax variants.

new_types: class Levenshtein(Expression, Func): arg_types = {'this': True, 'expression': True, 'max_distance': False} -- AST node for LEVENSHTEIN(str1, str2) calls
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Soundex and EditDistance follow minimal pattern: (1) Define class in expressions/string.py inheriting Expression and Func. (2) Parser recognizes LEVENSHTEIN token via TokenType enum (add if missing). (3) Generator provides levenshtein_sql(self, expression) -> str method returning target dialect syntax. (4) Dialect-specific generators override for variants (e.g., MySQL returns LEVENSHTEIN(...), Postgres may use custom UDF).
change_proposal.integration_point: Parser.FUNCTIONS dict (parser.py ~line 300-400) maps 'LEVENSHTEIN' string to parser lambda. Most dialects inherit default. Dialect-specific overrides in PostgresParser, MySQLParser, etc. if needed.
risks: TokenType enum expansion: ensure LEVENSHTEIN token doesn't conflict with reserved words in target dialects
Dialect variance: not all SQL dialects support LEVENSHTEIN natively; Postgres/MySQL do, BigQuery uses EDIT_DISTANCE; need per-dialect mapping in Generator
Function signature: some dialects use 2 args (str1, str2), others add threshold/max_distance; Levenshtein class design must support optional args
Performance: string distance on large datasets may be slow; no built-in optimization passes target string functions specifically

tool_usage.params: path=/tmp/sqlglot, summary=true, max_depth=2 -- high-level repo structure overview
path=/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/tokens.py, summary=true -- token system (Token, TokenType, Tokenizer classes)
path=/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot, symbol=parse, follow_depth=1 -- trace parse function definitions across modules (__init__, parser, dialects)
pattern=class Soundex|SOUNDEX|EDITDISTANCE, path=/tmp/sqlglot -- find existing string function patterns
grep -A 2 'class Soundex' sqlglot/expressions/string.py -- verify minimal class structure for string functions
head -50 sqlglot/expressions/string.py -- see string.py import and class definition patterns
find /tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot -type d -- enumerate subdirectories (dialects, optimizer, executor, expressions)
